D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Panorama Stitch & Blend — Geometry Is Not Enough

two frames perfectly registered in geometry still show a visible seam because their exposures differ; gain compensation + multi-band blend hide it

A panorama is built in two passes: register (find the homography that aligns overlapping frames) then blend (combine pixels in the overlap region). Even with perfect geometry, frames at slightly different exposures show a visible seam at the boundary. Brown & Lowe 2007 popularized gain compensation + multi-band blend to make the join invisible.

Two frames captured at slightly different exposures cover the same panorama. Cycle the blend mode: paste shows a hard seam, gain matches exposures globally, multi-band additionally hides residual seam by smoothing low-frequency mismatches in the overlap.
